name: Spaghetti Carbonara
tagline: Roman pasta with guanciale, egg, pecorino, black pepper — no cream
highlight: Real carbonara: egg, pork, cheese, pepper. No cream. Ever.

hero.alt: Carbonara coated with creamy egg sauce and crisp guanciale
hero.caption: carbonara — egg-yolk creamy

healthFacts: Carbonara's creaminess comes from emulsified egg yolk and pasta water — no cream needed.
Pecorino is sharper and saltier than parmesan — a little goes far.

steps.title: Render guanciale slowly
steps.detail: Cold pan, diced guanciale, medium heat. Render 8 minutes, stirring, until fat melts and the pieces are crisp on the edges.
steps.tip: Cold start is key — high heat torches the meat before fat releases.

steps.title: Boil and whisk
steps.detail: Boil spaghetti in salted water. Meanwhile whisk egg yolks, whole egg, and most of the pecorino with copious black pepper.
steps.tip: More pepper than feels reasonable. That's the dish.

steps.title: Combine off heat
steps.detail: Drain pasta (save water!). Off heat, toss pasta in the guanciale fat. Pour in egg-cheese mix and stir vigorously, splashing in pasta water until silky.
steps.tip: OFF heat. Hot pan = scrambled eggs. The pasta provides the heat.

steps.title: Serve immediately
steps.detail: Plate, top with the crisped guanciale and remaining pecorino. Eat now — carbonara waits for no one.
steps.tip: If your sauce thickens too much sitting on the plate, add a tablespoon of pasta water at the table.
